summary: Tested BUG #1 (created_by injection on eval POST), 'Mine only' filter on /evals, and the new group-run comments feature. BUG #1 fix is VERIFIED — POST /api/eval/jobs-with-es body now carries created_by=0ee59a27-... (interceptor fix in apiHelpers.js works). 'Mine only' toggle wires through and (a) flips variant, (b) renders chip + Clear-all, (c) re-issues list GET. However: (1) GET /api/eval/jobs has created_by=<uid> appended ALWAYS (even when Mine-only is OFF), because the same interceptor injects it on every GET — this contradicts the spec line 'Toggling OFF restores the full list (no created_by qp)'. (2) CRITICAL: the comments feature is BROKEN end-to-end because the evalApiClient (and the global eval axios instance) does NOT attach the session token to /api/eval/* requests. POST /api/eval/groups/<id>/comments returns 401 (verified in network log). The auth-token-as-query-param interceptor in the app only fires for /api/auth/*. As a result, create-comment + delete-comment will always 401 from the UI even with a valid localStorage session_token. The 'sign-in disabled' empty-state UI works, but the authenticated post path doesn't.

minor.issue: Returns HTTP 500 when expanding a real group on /evals (e.g. TEST_iter21_grp_2_7b5441f4-prod). Pre-existing, not part of this story but noisy in console.
Returns 500 alongside the aggregate failure for the same groupId.

ui_bugs.issue: NO session-token auth interceptor attached. /api/eval/* requests go out with no Authorization header, no cookie, and no ?access_token query param. Consequently POST /api/eval/groups/<id>/comments and DELETE /api/eval/groups/<id>/comments/<cid> return 401 from the UI even with a valid localStorage acm_session_token. Comments create + delete flow is broken end-to-end.

After the dead-`return` removal, the interceptor now always appends created_by on GET /api/eval/jobs (and group jobs). The 'Mine only' toggle's job-list filter is therefore indistinguishable from the default state at the network layer — toggling OFF still issues GET ...?created_by=<uid>. The spec calls for the qp to be present only when mineOnly is ON; today the qp is always present.

integration_issues.issue: POST /api/eval/groups/<groupId>/comments → 401 because no auth token attached to /api/eval/* axios instance. UI shows toast 'Sign in to post a comment' even though user IS signed in (PW Test User shown bottom-left of sidebar).
Same 401 root cause; could not verify delete because no own comment ever lands on the list.

verified_passing: BUG #1 — POST /api/eval/jobs-with-es body carries 'created_by':'0ee59a27-db9c-4647-aeee-f72173fcd757' (interceptor fix is working).
filter-mine-only-toggle is rendered, clickable, flips variant=default when ON, and adds the filter chip + 'Clear all' affordance.
Toggling Mine-only ON triggers a re-fetch GET /api/eval/jobs?limit=100&offset=0&created_by=<uid>.
GroupCommentsPanel renders inside the expanded Collapsible with data-testid group-comments-<groupId>.
Empty-state group-comments-empty-<groupId> renders when there are no comments.
Submit button (group-comment-submit-<groupId>) is disabled when text is empty, enables once non-whitespace text is typed.
POST body to comments endpoint is shaped correctly: {text:'...'} (verified via network interception).
action_items: Attach the session token to evalApiClient (and other /api/* clients that need auth). Either (a) install the same axios interceptor used for /api/auth/* — appending ?access_token=<localStorage.acm_session_token> — or (b) send Authorization: Bearer <token>. Server (_get_session_user) already accepts both. Without this fix, the entire comments create/delete feature is non-functional from the UI.

Make the created_by query-param injection on GET /api/eval/jobs conditional on filters.mineOnly. Either remove the interceptor for GET requests against /jobs and let EvalRuns.js pass created_by explicitly when mineOnly is true, OR scope attachOwnership matchers to POST/PUT/PATCH only.
Investigate the pre-existing 500 from /api/eval/jobs/aggregate and /api/eval/groups/{id}/jobs — not part of this story but the testing-agent saw it while exercising the comments panel.
critical_code_review_comments: apiHelpers.attachOwnership currently injects created_by on EVERY method (GET/DELETE as query param). That conflates 'identity stamping' (POST body) with 'ownership filtering' (GET query). Recommend splitting: only stamp body on write methods, and let callers opt-in to ?created_by on reads.

evalApi.js has no auth interceptor. Given several /api/eval/* routes are protected (comments POST/DELETE, possibly more), the auth concern should live in a shared module rather than being /api/auth/* specific.

GroupCommentsPanel: handlePost has a partial 401 mapping ('Sign in to post a comment') but the user IS signed in — the message is misleading. After fixing the auth interceptor, this branch should be dead code for authenticated users.

success_rate.frontend: 60% — Mine-only + interceptor BUG-fix verified; comments create/delete blocked by missing auth

seed_data_creation: None. Used existing seeded session + existing prod groups (e.g. TEST_iter21_grp_2_7b5441f4-prod).
context_for_next_testing_agent: After the main agent attaches the session token to evalApiClient (and ideally other /api/* clients), re-run: open /evals with localStorage acm_session_token=pw_emergent_gate_post; expand any non-_ungrouped group; verify POST /api/eval/groups/<id>/comments returns 200 and the new row appears with 'you' badge + Trash icon; click Trash and verify DELETE returns 200 and the row vanishes; toggle Mine-only OFF and confirm the next GET /api/eval/jobs has NO created_by qp. The BUG #1 fix on POST is already verified — no need to re-verify unless the apiHelpers file is touched.
rca of the issue: Frontend bug #2 (comments 401): /api/eval/* requests bypass the auth-token attachment that only fires for /api/auth/*. _get_session_user falls back to cookie/Authorization/?access_token, but the frontend supplies none of those for /api/eval/* — so POST/DELETE on protected eval routes always 401. Reproduction: localStorage.setItem('acm_session_token','pw_emergent_gate_post'); GET /evals; expand a group; type a comment; click Post → 401 in Network. Mitigation: add the same access_token query-param interceptor (or an Authorization-header one) to evalApiClient (and any other axios clients in services/) so the token rides on every request once the user is authenticated.
